questions.questionText: The method based on rise in boiling point is applied to find the molecular masses of
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement I : A 0.1 m solution of urea shows a lower boiling point than a 0.1 m solution of KCl.

Statement II : The increase in boiling point is directly related to the total number of particles present in the solution.
options: If both statements are TRUE, and Statement II gives the correct reason for Statement I.
If both statements are TRUE, but Statement II does not give the correct reason for Statement I.
If Statement I is TRUE, but Statement II is FALSE.
If Statement I is FALSE, but Statement II is TRUE.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: When dissolved in a given solvent, a solute 'S' shows reversible association to form trimers. A 0.1 molal solution of this solute produces the same elevation in boiling point as a 0.08 molal solution of another solute which does not associate or dissociate. What percentage of solute 'S' has formed trimers?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A sample of sea water contains 5.85% NaCl and 9.50% MgCl2 by mass of the solution. Find its normal boiling point, taking 80% ionisation for NaCl and 50% ionisation for MgCl2 ($K_b$(H2O) = 0.51 kg mol-1 K).
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: PtCl4·6H2O may be represented as a hydrated coordination compound. A 1 molal aqueous solution shows a freezing point depression of 3.72°. If ionisation is complete and $K_f$(H2O) = 1.86° mol–1 kg, then the complex is –
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following sequences shows the freezing point in descending order?
options: 0.05 M KNO3 > 0.04 M CaCl2 > 0.140 M sugar > 0.075 M CuSO4
0.04 M BaCl2 > 0.140 M sucrose > 0.075 M CuSO4 > 0.05 M KNO3
0.075 M CuSO4 > 0.140 M sucrose > 0.04 M BaCl2 > 0.05 M KNO3
0.075 M CuSO4 > 0.05 M NaNO3 > 0.140 M sucrose > 0.04 M BaCl2
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An iron–cyanide complex undergoes complete ionisation in a 1m (molal) solution. The observed elevation in boiling point is 2.08. The complex is (Kb = 0.52° mol–1 kg) :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A solution prepared by dissolving 1 g of urea in water shows a boiling point of 100.25°C. If 3 g of glucose is dissolved in an identical volume of water, what will be the boiling point of the resulting solution?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
Statement I : The depression in the freezing point for a 0.1 m aqueous solution of glucose is greater than that observed for a 0.1 m aqueous solution of urea.

Statement II : The value of the molal depression constant ($k_{\mathrm{f}}$) remains identical for both solutions.
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are CORRECT, and Statement II provides the correct reasoning for Statement I.
Both Statement I and Statement II are CORRECT, but Statement II does not provide the correct reasoning for Statement I.
Statement I is CORRECT, but Statement II is INCORRECT.
Statement I is INCORRECT, but Statement II is CORRECT.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Which of the following colligative properties is most suitable for calculating the molar masses of macromolecules like proteins and polymers?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A non-ionising solid has to be identified by its molecular mass. When 10 g of this solid is dissolved in 100 g of water, the freezing point of the solution becomes $-1.24^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. Calculate its molecular mass. $K_{\mathrm{f}}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)=1.86^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \mathrm{kg} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}$.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The same mass of two solutions in water is taken and mixed. One solution contains 3.6 g glucose in 100 g water, while the other contains 3.6 g urea in 100 g water. If the freezing point of the solution formed is $-x^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, find the value of ' $1000 x$ ' ( $K_{\mathrm{f}}$ of water $=1.86$ )
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The boiling point elevation constant for toluene is $3.32 \mathrm{~K} \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. The normal boiling point of toluene is $110.7^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$. The enthalpy of vaporisation of toluene (in $\mathrm{kJ} / \mathrm{mol}$ ) would be nearly.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: For [ $\mathrm{CrCl}_3 . \times \mathrm{NH}_3$ ], elevation in boiling point of one molal solution is triple of one molal aqueous solution of urea. Assuming $100 \%$ ionisation of complex molecule and coordination number as six, calculate the value of x .
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText:
A solution is prepared by adding 18 g of glucose $\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_6\right)$ to 1 kg of water. When this solution is brought to $-0.2325^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, what mass of ice will separate out?

$\left[\right.$ Given : $\left.K_f\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)=1.86 \mathrm{~K}-\mathrm{kg} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}\right]$
questions.subject: Chemistry
